page.name: IRS-UPDATE-----Acceptance-Agent-Application-Moratorium-----Update
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
Forwarded Message
============ Forwarded message ============
From: *ITIN Program Office <itinprogramoffice@irs.gov>
To: <Undisclosed recipients:>
Date: Thu, 21 Dec 2023 18:41:52 -0500
Subject: Acceptance Agent Application Moratorium - Update
============ Forwarded message ============

Dear Acceptance Agent:

You are receiving this email as a participant in the IRS Acceptance Agent Program whose agreement is expiring December 31, 2023. As you know, the IRS imposed a moratorium on the Acceptance Agent Program beginning August 15, 2022, to allow for significant modernization efforts resulting in a more efficient application process. During this time, Form 13551, Application to Participate in the IRS Acceptance Agent Program cannot be accepted and will be returned.

Despite your expiring Acceptance Agent (AA) or Certifying Acceptance Agent (CAA) agreement, you will be able to continue to serve as an AA or CAA under your current agreement and submit Form W-7, Application for IRS Individual Taxpayer Identification Number for your clients until the new system is in place. Once the moratorium ends, a notification of rescission will be issued and you will be invited to submit your renewal application via the new process. In preparation, please ensure you and your authorized users complete the mandatory ITIN Acceptance Agent Training, and that CAAs also complete the forensic document training.

We anticipate the modernized application process will be complete in January 2024 and look forward to your continued participation.

Howard Gordon
Section Chief, ITIN Policy Section
Internal Revenue Service

DISCLAIMER: This email and any attachments are confidential and may be privileged or otherwise protected from disclosure. If you are not the intended recipient, please immediately delete the email and any attachments, and notify the sender. Any other use of this e-mail is prohibited. We do not guarantee that this email or its attachments are free from viruses or other defects. It is up to the recipient to ensure it is virus-free. We do not accept liability or responsibility for any errors or omissions in the contents of this message, which arise as a result of its transmission.
```

page.name: 2024.01.11-Preparación-de-Fin-de-Año--Contable-y-Calendario-Fiscal-Próximo
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
Year-End Bookkeeping and Tax Schedule

Importante: Preparación de Fin de Año Contable y Calendario Fiscal Próximo

Estimado/a [Nombre del Cliente],

A medida que nos acercamos al final del año, es crucial que comencemos a prepararnos para su revisión financiera de fin de año y las obligaciones fiscales venideras...

Lista de Verificación para el Cierre Contable de Fin de Año:

  • Recibos: Compilar todos los recibos de compras relacionadas con el negocio.
  • Facturas: Recopilar copias de las facturas de ventas emitidas y recibidas.
  • Estados de Cuenta Bancarios: Asegurarse de que todos los estados de cuenta bancarios estén actualizados y accesibles.
  • Estados de Cuenta de Tarjetas de Crédito: Recoger los estados de cuenta de los gastos de tarjetas de crédito empresariales.
  • Estados de Cuenta de Préstamos: Si corresponde, incluir los estados de cuenta de cualquier préstamo empresarial.
  • Registros de Nómina: Recopilar información completa de la nómina del año.
  • Documentos Tributarios: Preparar cualquier formulario de impuestos o correspondencia recibida durante el año.
  • Compras de Activos: Proporcionar documentación para cualquier activo o equipo nuevo comprado.
  • Registros de Inventario: Si corresponde, proporcionar un recuento y valoración actual del inventario.
  • Documentos Adicionales: Incluir cualquier otro documento o informe financiero relevante.

Nuestro Apoyo:

Estamos aquí para asistirle a lo largo de este proceso. Nuestro equipo está listo para responder cualquier pregunta y ofrecer ayuda con la preparación de documentos o cualquier consulta financiera.

Nuestro Calendario para Enero - Abril de 2024 Enero 2024:

  • 01/11: Comenzar el procesamiento de las declaraciones de impuestos sobre ventas.
  • 01/12-01/13: Continuar el procesamiento de las declaraciones de impuestos sobre ventas.
  • 01/19:Comunicación sobre las presentaciones de impuestos estimados.
  • 01/20: Asegurarse de que todos los impuestos estimados estén presentados.
  • 01/21: Comenzar la preparación de los Formularios 1099-NEC. 01/27: Prepararse para las fechas límite del 31/01.
  • Febrero 2024: 02/01: Presentar los formularios que vencen el 31/01.
  • 02/02-02/03: Comenzar la revisión de los documentos fiscales de sociedades y S Corp.
  • 02/08-02/09: Trabajar en las declaraciones de impuestos individuales (1040).
  • 02/10: Finalizar un lote de declaraciones de impuestos individuales.
  • Marzo 2024: 03/01-03/02: Finalizar y presentar las declaraciones de sociedades y S Corp.
  • 03/03-03/04: Continuar trabajando en las declaraciones individuales y prepararse para el período pico.
  • Abril 2024: 04/05-04/07: Finalizar las declaraciones de impuestos individuales y corporativos.
  • 04/08: Presentar todas las declaraciones de impuestos que vencen el 15/04.
  • 04/12: Comenzar las revisiones y planificación postemporada fiscal.

Conclusión: Su cooperación en proporcionar los documentos requeridos lo antes posible es invaluable para un cierre exitoso de fin de año. Estamos comprometidos a apoyarlo en cada paso de este proceso. Gracias por su pronta atención a este asunto. Esperamos asegurar una finalización eficiente y exhaustiva de su cierre financiero de fin de año y las próximas obligaciones fiscales.

Saludos cordiales,

Cody Rice-Velasquez

CEO

317.756.9969

page.name: New-Check---IN
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
Check-In Workflow Using Zoho Forms

Check-In Workflow Using Zoho Forms

Problem:

Clients not consistently receiving the policies.

Solutions:

  • Policies have been updated and streamlined on our website.
  • By checking in, clients automatically receive a notification, thus accepting our conditions.
  • Reduces paper waste, man hours handling papers, uploading, and entering, reduces storage, which in the long term reduces costs.
  • The process is seamless, easy, and effective for both client and staff.

Overview:

This workflow outlines the process for checking in users through a form on our website, determining if they are new or returning based on their email or phone number, and the subsequent steps following their identification.

Workflow Steps:

Form Setup and Integration

  • A check-in form is developed using Zoho Forms.
  • This form is placed on the website under the "Check-In" section.

User Submission Process

  • Users can check in using either their email or phone number.
  • The workflow begins once the form is submitted.

Contact Information Lookup

  • The system searches for the user's contact details in our database.

Handling Non-Existing Contacts

  • If the contact is not found and a phone number is provided:
  • A text message is sent using our Twilio text line (317-7661) with a link to a web form to create an account.
  • A Click notification is set up to inform the team of new account creation.

Response to Existing Contacts

  • If the contact is found:
  • A text message is sent via the Twilio line informing them of our terms of use, service, and privacy policy, stating that by using our services, they agree to these terms.
  • Following this, an email is sent with additional information and a request to update any outdated details.

Final Confirmation and Data Logging

  • The user receives a final confirmation with their ticket ID number.
  • An entry is made in the "Check-In Module" for tracking purposes.

Additional Considerations:

  • Ensure mapping of form fields to CRM fields is accurate for seamless data integration.
  • Leverage Zoho's automation features for text and email communications.
  • Update the workflow periodically to enhance its effectiveness and accuracy.
Access Zoho Forms

page.name: 01.2-External
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id

page.name: 01.2.1-FAQ-Section
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

1. What do I need for an ITIN?

Answer: To apply for an ITIN, you need to complete your tax return and submit Form W-7 (Application for IRS Individual Taxpayer Identification Number). Additionally, identification documentation is required, preferably a passport. If a passport is not available, another government-issued ID (not expired) along with your birth certificate will be needed.

Service Inclusion: Our service includes the completion of all necessary forms, certification of a copy of your document, and ensuring that the original document is not mailed out, maintaining its safety.

Recommended Document: A passport is the easiest and most efficient form of identification for this process.

Processing Time: The processing time for an ITIN is approximately 120 days.

Fee: The fee for this service is $300.

2. How do I register a business?

Answer: To register a business, you should have your ITIN or SSN. Our service includes both the state registration fees and applications for EIN. State certifications are required for the EIN, and the names must match on both. The state registration process takes 1-2 business days, while obtaining an EIN can take 1-2 weeks. However, there have been instances where the IRS has missed applications and not processed them, requiring us to resubmit multiple times, which greatly extends the processing time. We will continue to submit the application until an answer is received.

Fees: The total price for this service is $300. If you already have your state registration, we charge $100 for the EIN. If you already have the EIN, we charge $200 for the state registration.

3. How do I plate a vehicle in Indiana?

Answer: To plate a vehicle in Indiana, you must have a social security number and a driver's license. Alternatively, a vehicle can be plated by an active business with a valid EIN. The cost for our service is $300 plus BMV fees. We will need the date of purchase, purchase price, and VIN to estimate the BMV fees. Additionally, active insurance is required, which we can quote in our office. The deposit for insurance typically ranges between $100-$300, depending on your driving history.

Required Documents: The necessary documents include the Title (or Dealer documentation like title application with barcode, bill of sale, ST108 form, etc.). We will complete the documents here in the office and recommend not filling out anything other than the seller signature, if applicable.

Timeline: You have 45 days from the purchase date to plate the vehicle. On average, we can have your temporary plates ready in 1-3 business weeks, and your metal plate will follow 1-2 weeks after. The title will be mailed directly to you or the lien holder.

page.name: 01.2.2-History
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
About SRV Group
“Everyone on the team plays an equal role. My role is to create the wave and everyone on our team keeps the wave going.” — WILLIAM WANG, FOUNDER, VIZIO

History

In January 2019, SRV Group was born in Event Mega Mall. Started by Cody Rice-Velasquez and their husband, we began as a small venture offering only one or two services. Despite humble beginnings and zero clients, the grit and determination of our founding team laid a strong foundation. Our first-year revenue barely touched $39,000.

In just a year, that revenue doubled. By our third year, buoyed by expanding our financial offerings, we soared to an impressive $1.4 million in revenue. At our peak, our team grew to 11 full-time staff operating out of two strategic locations.

The start of 2023 has been challenging, but we're far from discouraged. With a strategic plan in place, we're primed for exponential growth, fully committed to realizing our full potential.

Cody Rice-Velasquez, Founder and CEO

"We all have the power to make a positive impact in the world. Working together is the best way to ensure that our collective actions have a positive effect on the future. Let's be there for each other, no matter the circumstances. We can all achieve great things without bringing each other down. Let's strive to be the change we want to see and to leave the world better than we found it. Kindness and compassion are essential in our interactions with others."

Our Mission

To empower the community with financial resources and knowledge while respecting their dignity. We aim to be a one-stop-shop for financial and multi-service needs, utilizing innovation and excellence to make a lasting impact. Our commitment goes beyond business; it's about building a legacy of trust, service, and community upliftment.

Our Values and Beliefs

  • Integrity
  • Collaboration
  • Excellence
  • Honesty
  • Ownership
  • Unity
  • Quality
  • Innovation

Stay in the loop

Sign up with your email address to receive news and updates.

page.name: 01.2.3-SRVGroup.co-Terms-of-Use
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
SRV Group Terms of Use

Acceptance of Terms

By accessing or using the services and devices provided by SRVGroup.co, a subsidiary of SRV Capital Holdings Inc., in collaboration with Servicios Rice-Velasquez Tax & MultiServices LLC, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Use and Service.

Use of Services and Devices

Services include tax preparation, financial planning, and related solutions. Devices are strictly for business purposes, adhering to SRV Group's professional standards and values.

User Conduct and Responsibilities

Use services and devices lawfully and provide accurate, non-misleading information. Devices should be used responsibly, with adherence to security protocols.

Payment and Fees

Services rendered are subject to fees outlined in consultation or service contracts. Payments are due upon invoice receipt. Sales are non-refundable once services commence.

Security and Privacy

SRV Group monitors device activity. Users consent to such monitoring. Report any loss, theft, or unauthorized use of devices immediately.

Maintenance and Support

Adhere to guidelines for device care. Contact IT for support, avoiding unauthorized repairs or alterations.

Termination

SRV Group may terminate access for violation of these terms, with or without notice.

Return of Devices

Return devices in good condition, minus normal wear and tear, upon termination of employment or upon request.

Amendments

SRV Group reserves the right to amend these terms. Continued use after changes constitutes consent to revised terms.

Governing Law

Governed by the laws of the State in which SRV Group operates.

Access SRV Group Services

page.name: 01.2.4-SRVGRoup.co-Privacy-Policy
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
SRV Group Privacy Policy

Introduction

This Privacy Policy applies to all users of SRV Group's services. It outlines how we collect, use, share, and protect personal information.

Information Collection

  • Personal Data: Including name, address, contact details, Social Security number, and financial information for tax and multiservice purposes.
  • Technical Data: Such as IP address, device type, operating system, browser, and service interaction.

Use of Information

To provide, improve, and personalize services. For transaction processing and compliance with tax laws and regulations.

Sharing of Information

No selling or sharing of personal information with third parties for marketing. Information may be shared with legal advisors, tax authorities, and as required by law.

Security Measures

Appropriate measures are taken to protect personal information against unauthorized access and misuse.

Changes to the Policy

SRV Group may modify this policy, with significant changes notified to users.

Contact

For queries regarding the Privacy Policy, contact info@SRVGROUP.CO.

Access SRV Group Privacy Policy

page.name: 01.2.5---Terms---of---Service
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
TÉRMINOS Y CONDICIONES DE SERVICIO "R.E.G.L.A.S."

REEMBOLSOS NO DISPONIBLES.
  • ¡Estamos aquí para ayudarte a completar y enviar formularios de manera eficiente! 
  • Tu pago es por nuestro tiempo de tramitar tu solicitud y no garantiza la rapidez o el éxito de tu trámite con la agencia de destino.
  • No se concederán reembolso por el servio o nuestra tiempo invertido, sin importar el estatus de trámite.
  • Mucho menos brindara reembolso ni en parte a quienes, por su falta de entendimiento sobre los servicios acordadas y sus costos-ya claramente explicados-los solicitan. 
  • Nos reservemos el derecho de negar terminar el servicio o brindar futuros servicios.
EFICIENCIA
  • ¿Experimentas retrasos? Recomendamos hablar con la agencia correspondiente (Estado; IRS; BMV etc). 
  • una vez que hemos enviado tu trámite nuestro compromiso se limita a ese punto.
  • Somos seres humanos no máquinas favor de ser realista en su esperanzas. 
  • El día que se entrega sus documentos no es necesariamente el día que lo empezamos.
  • Si ocupa algo para cierta fecha entregarlo lo antes posible con anticipo y recargas por rapidez se cobrará minio $100.
NO HAY GARANTÍAS
  • No somos abogados, por lo que no podemos asegurar el éxito de tu solicitud.
  • Ofrecemos llenar y tramitar varias asuntos pero NO somos el agencia.
  • No hay garantías. Pero si usted es razonable y respetuoso haremos lo que nos alcanza para apoyarle.
LÍMITES DE RESPONSABILIDAD
  • Nuestra tarifa cubre nuestro tiempo y asistencia, pero no garantiza resultados específicos.
  • No hay reembolso.
  • No hay garantías..
ACTITUD
  • Valoramos el respeto y la dignidad en todas nuestras interacciones, y esperamos lo mismo de nuestros clientes.
  • No hay razon de faltarnos el respeto.
  • Si nos grita, habla en mala forma, faltarnos el respecto, nos interrumpe, pasa sin permiso o cualquier cosa que no es aceptado por las empresa serás removido por seguridad.
SOSIEGO
  • Cody tiene una alta carga de trabajo que requiere concentración. Por favor, no insistas en verlo si no está disponible.
  • Es común que le llegan más que 300 mensajes en un día. Sea paciente.
  • No manda varias mensajes ni llamadas.
  • Mejor es mandar un email detallada con todo el info y archivos la primera vez.
CONFIDENCIALIDAD
Tus datos y la información relacionada con tus trámites serán tratados con la máxima confidencialidad. No compartiremos tus detalles con terceros sin tu consentimiento expreso.

COMUNICACIÓN
La comunicación principal se llevará a cabo por correo electrónico o mediante los canales de contacto proporcionados. Es importante que estemos en contacto para cualquier actualización o información relevante.

PAGOS
Los pagos por nuestros servicios deben realizarse de acuerdo con los términos establecidos en la factura o acuerdo de pago. Cualquier demora en el pago puede afectar la continuación de nuestros servicios.

CANCELACIONES
Si deseas cancelar nuestros servicios antes de que se haya completado tu trámite, comunícate con nosotros para discutir los términos y posibles reembolsos parciales, si corresponde.

MODIFICACIONES DE LOS TÉRMINOS
Nos reservamos el derecho de modificar estos términos y condiciones en cualquier momento. Te notificaremos de cualquier cambio importante y tendrás la oportunidad de revisar y aceptar los nuevos términos antes de continuar.

DECLARACIÓN
Al utilizar nuestros servicios en Servicios Rice-Velasquez Tax & Multiservice LLC (SRVTM), SRV Property Management LLC (SRVPM), o cualquier otra entidad relacionada con SRV Capital Holdings Inc., aceptas y estás de acuerdo con los términos y condiciones establecidos anteriormente y en nuestro sitio web. Por favor, asegúrate de leer y comprender completamente estos términos antes de utilizar nuestros servicios. Si tienes alguna pregunta o inquietud, no dudes en contactarnos al email help@srvpro.zohodesk.com

Atentamente,

Cody Rice-Velasquez, Founder and CEO
CEO de SRV Capital Holdings Inc. y Face de todas nuestras empresas.

page.name: A2-Documents-and-Templates
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
Documents & Templates

Introduction: The Documents & Templates section serves as your go-to repository for all essential documents and templates required for seamless operations. Whether you need a standard form for client engagements, work-related templates, or personal document formats, this space streamlines your access to high-quality, SRV-approved resources. Explore to enhance your efficiency and maintain consistency across all documents.

Keywords/Tags: Templates, Document Management, Workflows, Efficiency Tools

page.name: 02.1-Services-Offered-by-SRV-Group
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
Service Categories

A. Tax Services

Services related to preperation and the filing of taxes and other tax requirements

B. Financial Services

Our financial services include accounting, tax preparation, and more, designed to keep your finances in check and compliant with regulations.

C. Subscription Services

Recurring services and memberships that provide ongoing benefits, including internet subscriptions and facility access, for our valued clients.

D. Indiana Services

Services related to the Indiana Bureau of Motor Vehicles, Secretary of State, and other local services essential for residents and businesses in Indiana.

E. Document Preparation

From simple letters to complex legal documents, our document preparation services cater to all your needs with precision and expertise.

F. Consulting Services

Expert advisory services across various domains, including immigration, financial planning, and more, to guide you through complex processes.

G. Printing Services

High-quality printing services ranging from basic document printing to advanced and custom print solutions, catering to all your printing needs.

H. Cargo Services

Comprehensive cargo and shipping options covering various destinations and package sizes, ensuring your items reach safely and on time.

I. Miscellaneous Services

A variety of other services that cater to unique needs, ensuring you have access to comprehensive solutions under one roof.

page.name: 02.1.2-TÉRMINOS-Y-CONDICIONES-DE-SERVICIO---"R.E.G.L.A.S."
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
TÉRMINOS Y CONDICIONES DE SERVICIO - "R.E.G.L.A.S."

Partes: Este Acuerdo de Servicio o Terminos y Condiciones de Servicio - "R.E.G.L.A.S." ("Acuerdo") es entre SRV Capital Holdings Inc. ("SRVGroup.co") its subsidiaries, officers, agents, etc. y el Cliente.

R - Reembolsos, Precios y Cargos

  • REEMBOLSOS NO DISPONIBLES.
  • Tu pago es por nuestro tiempo de tramitar tu solicitud y no garantiza la rapidez o el éxito de tu trámite con la agencia de destino.
  • No se concederán reembolsos por el servicio o nuestro tiempo invertido, sin importar el estatus de trámite.
  • Mucho menos brindará reembolso ni en parte a quienes, por su falta de entendimiento sobre los servicios acordados y sus costos-ya claramente explicados-los solicitan.
  • Nos reservamos el derecho de negar terminar el servicio o brindar futuros servicios.
  • Todos los pagos por servicios son finales y no reembolsables.
  • Nuestros precios reflejan el tiempo y recursos dedicados a procesar tu solicitud.
  • Los cargos adicionales, como tarifas por servicios urgentes o cargos por mora, se aplicarán según se especifica en nuestra política de precios.
Cargos y Términos de Pago de Contabilidad Mensual
  • Nuestros servicios de contabilidad mensual se estructuran en torno a un acuerdo de servicio anual, dividido en pagos mensuales para la conveniencia del cliente. 
  • Este enfoque permite a los clientes gestionar sus finanzas de manera más efectiva, con la opción de pagar todo el año por adelantado si lo prefieren. 
  • Es esencial entender los siguientes términos y condiciones de pago:
    • **Fecha de Pago Debido**: 
      • Los pagos por nuestros servicios vencen el 1 de cada mes. Los clientes son responsables de asegurar pagos oportunos. 
      • Si notas que tu pago no ha sido procesado para el 1, se deben hacer arreglos inmediatos para cumplir con tu obligación de pago.
    • **Política de Pagos Atrasados**: 
      • Se proporciona un período de gracia hasta el 5 del mes. 
      • Después del 5, los pagos atrasados incurrirán en una tarifa de $20, más los intereses aplicables. 
      • La falta de pago dentro de los 31 días podría resultar en que tu cuenta sea enviada a cobranzas, y los servicios podrían cesar después de 15 días sin pago. 
      • Para información detallada, por favor refiérete a tu acuerdo de servicio.
    • **Pago Completo por Otros Servicios**: 
      • Para servicios fuera de la contabilidad mensual, se requiere el pago completo por adelantado, incluyendo cualquier tarifa de tarjeta de crédito. 
      • Nuestros precios son no negociables, y no se proporcionarán descuentos.
    • **Cargos por Retrasos y Envíos No Relacionados con el Negocio**: 
      • Si los retrasos de un cliente nos causan posponer servicios para otros clientes, se cobrará una tarifa de hasta $100 por ocurrencia. 
      • Los clientes que envíen recibos o documentos no relacionados con su negocio, según nuestro acuerdo, incurrirán en cargos a nuestra tarifa de costo, actualmente $60 por hora o aproximadamente $2 por transacción. 
      • Esto enfatiza la importancia de proporcionar solo información precisa y relevante. 
      • Mezclar gastos personales con gastos de negocios también resultará en cargos adicionales debido al trabajo administrativo extra involucrado.

E - Eficiencia y Tiempo

  • Nos comprometemos a manejar tus solicitudes con la mayor eficiencia posible.
  • Los tiempos de procesamiento dependen de varios factores, incluyendo la complejidad del servicio y la cooperación del cliente en proporcionar la documentación necesaria de manera oportuna.
  • ¿Experimentas retrasos? Recomendamos hablar con la agencia correspondiente (Estado; IRS; BMV etc).
  • Una vez que hemos enviado tu trámite nuestro compromiso se limita a ese punto.
  • Somos seres humanos no máquinas favor de ser realista en sus esperanzas.
  • El día que se entrega tus documentos no es necesariamente el día que lo empezamos.
  • Si ocupa algo para cierta fecha entregarlo lo antes posible con anticipo y recargas por rapidez se cobrará minio $100.
Para garantizar un procesamiento transparente y eficiente de tus solicitudes, estamos implementando un sistema estructurado de envío y priorización. 
Por favor, lee cuidadosamente las siguientes pautas:
  • **Número de ID de Envío**: 
    • Al entregar documentos o realizar cualquier envío, es obligatorio solicitar un Número de ID de Envío. 
    • Este número es crucial para el seguimiento de tus documentos y solicitudes durante el período de procesamiento.
  • **Fecha en los Documentos**: 
    • Cada documento o envío debe ir acompañado de una fecha. 
    • Esta fecha se utilizará para determinar tu posición en nuestra cola de procesamiento.
  • **Prioridad Basada en la Fecha de Envío**: 
    • Nuestro procesamiento se adherirá estrictamente al orden de las fechas de envío. 
    • Abordaremos los documentos y solicitudes hasta una fecha determinada antes de pasar a los envíos de fechas posteriores.
  • **Actualizaciones del Sitio Web**: 
    • Para mantenerte informado, actualizaremos regularmente nuestro sitio web con las fechas en las que estamos trabajando actualmente. 
    • Esta transparencia tiene como objetivo darte una comprensión clara de nuestro cronograma de procesamiento.
    • **Tiempos de Espera Estimados**: 
      • Estamos comprometidos a desarrollar una fórmula que te proporcionará un tiempo de espera promedio basado en los volúmenes actuales de envío y las capacidades de procesamiento. 
      • Esta estimación estará disponible para ayudar a establecer expectativas realistas con respecto al tiempo de respuesta para tus solicitudes.
Siguiendo estas pautas, nos ayudas a mantener un sistema de procesamiento justo y eficiente para todos nuestros clientes. Tu cooperación y comprensión son muy apreciadas mientras nos esforzamos por atenderte mejor.
 

G - Garantías y Descargos de Responsabilidad

  • No ofrecemos garantías sobre los resultados específicos de los servicios proporcionados.
  • Nuestra responsabilidad se limita a la prestación de servicios con diligencia y profesionalismo, sin garantía de éxito en procedimientos o aplicaciones específicas.
  • No somos abogados, por lo que no podemos asegurar el éxito de tu solicitud.
  • Ofrecemos llenar y tramitar varios asuntos pero NO somos la agencia.
  • No hay garantías. Pero si usted es razonable y respetuoso haremos lo que nos alcanza para apoyarle.
  • Nuestra información es con fines educativos y no debe interpretarse como asesoramiento legal. 
  • Instamos a la consulta con abogados calificados para asuntos legales.

L - Límites de Nuestros Servicios y Responsabilidad Limitada

  • Nuestros servicios se limitan a la asesoría, preparación y presentación de documentos según los requerimientos del cliente.
  • No somos responsables de decisiones o acciones tomadas por terceros, incluidas agencias o instituciones con las que se interactúe.
  • LÍMITES DE RESPONSABILIDAD: 
    • Nuestra tarifa cubre nuestro tiempo y asistencia, pero no garantiza resultados específicos.
  • No hay reembolso. 
  • No hay garantías.

A - Actitud y Comportamiento del Cliente

  • Esperamos que todas las interacciones con nuestro personal y en nuestras oficinas se realicen con respeto y profesionalismo.
  • No toleraremos comportamientos abusivos, difamatorios o irrespetuosos.
  • La violación de esta política puede resultar en la terminación de los servicios.
  • Valoramos el respeto y la dignidad en todas nuestras interacciones, y esperamos lo mismo de nuestros clientes.
  • No hay razón de faltarnos el respeto. Si nos grita, habla en mala forma, faltarnos el respecto, nos interrumpe, pasa sin permiso o cualquier cosa que no es aceptado por la empresa serás removido por seguridad.
Política de No Desprestigio y Conducta
  • Mantener un ambiente respetuoso y profesional para nuestros clientes y personal es de máxima importancia. Para asegurar esto, hemos establecido las siguientes pautas sobre el comportamiento y la comunicación de los clientes:
    • Prohibición del Desprestigio**: 
      • Se prohíbe estrictamente a los clientes hacer declaraciones despectivas o falsas sobre nuestros servicios, personal o prácticas comerciales. 
      • Esto incluye, pero no se limita a, arrebatos verbales en nuestra oficina, declaraciones engañosas o falsas hechas a otros clientes, o cualquier forma de difamación pública.
    • Proceso Constructivo de Quejas**: 
      • Damos la bienvenida y alentamos la retroalimentación, incluyendo la crítica constructiva. 
      • Si tienes una queja o problema, solicitamos que se envíe a través de nuestro formulario de envío de quejas disponible en línea en nuestro servicio de ayuda. 
      • Esto asegura que tus preocupaciones se aborden de manera rápida y adecuada de manera que pueda llevar a resultados productivos.
    • Penalizaciones por Violación**: 
      • En caso de una violación de esta política, se cobrará una tarifa mínima de $100 por acciones o declaraciones despectivas. 
      • Además, en casos donde ocurra calumnia o difamación, nos reservamos el derecho de emprender acciones legales en la máxima medida permitida por la ley. 
      • Esto es para proteger la integridad de nuestro negocio y el bienestar de nuestro personal y clientela.
Al adherirse a estas pautas, podemos asegurar un ambiente positivo y profesional para todos los involucrados. Es nuestro compromiso abordar todas las preocupaciones de manera constructiva, y pedimos tu cooperación para mantener el respeto y la dignidad de nuestra comunidad. Exigimos respeto y educación en todas las interacciones, comprometiéndonos a proveer un servicio de calidad.

S - Sosiego y Expectativas Racionales

  • Pedimos a nuestros clientes que mantengan expectativas realistas respecto a los tiempos de procesamiento y resultados.
  • Entendemos la importancia de cada solicitud y trabajamos diligentemente para cumplir con los plazos.
  • Sin embargo, es crucial reconocer que algunos procesos pueden llevar más tiempo de lo esperado.
  • Cody tiene una alta carga de trabajo que requiere concentración. Por favor, no insistas en verlo si no está disponible.
  • Es común que le llegan más que 300 mensajes en un día. Sea paciente.
  • No mande varias mensajes ni llamadas. Mejor es mandar un email detallado con toda la información y archivos la primera vez.
  • La comunicación principal se llevará a cabo por correo electrónico o mediante los canales de contacto proporcionados. 
  • Es importante que estemos en contacto para cualquier actualización o información relevante.
  • Reconocemos los desafíos de interactuar con agencias gubernamentales. 
  • Nuestra experiencia nos permite representarles eficazmente, aunque los tiempos de espera y resultados pueden variar y están fuera de nuestro control.

Confidencialidad

  • Tus datos y la información relacionada con tus trámites serán tratados con la máxima confidencialidad. No compartiremos tus detalles con terceros sin tu consentimiento expreso.
  • Nos comprometemos a mantener la confidencialidad de toda la información proporcionada por nuestros clientes y a no divulgarla a terceros sin el consentimiento explícito del cliente, excepto cuando lo exija la ley.

Privacidad

  • Nuestra política de privacidad detalla cómo recopilamos, usamos y protegemos la información personal de nuestros clientes.
  • Estamos comprometidos con la protección de tu privacidad y la seguridad de tus datos.

Términos de Uso

  • Al utilizar nuestros servicios, el cliente acepta cumplir con los términos y condiciones establecidos en este acuerdo.
  • Nos reservamos el derecho de modificar estos términos en cualquier momento, con aviso previo a nuestros clientes.

Declaración y Reconocimiento

  • Al firmar este acuerdo, el cliente reconoce haber leído, entendido y aceptado los términos y condiciones aquí presentados.
  • Además, el cliente se compromete a seguir las políticas de conducta y colaborar con el proceso establecido para la prestación de servicios.

3. Click on Create Appointment

4. Type "juan ramon"

5. Click on Juan Ramon

6. Click on dropdown trigger

7. Select the Appropriate Service

8. Click on MMM D, YYYY

9. Select the Date and Time

10. Time

11. Add the Rep

14. Click on Save

15. Open Service Appointment

16. Attache Necessary Documents

17. Click on Upload File

18. Click on Click here or drop files here to attach.

19. Select "20240125_juan ramon certificado para traducir.jpg" from file upload menu

20. Click on Attach

21. Add other info as needed.

22. Click on "Check Box to Save"

23. Click on Add Job sheet

24. Add Job Sheet Details, this will create the service job in the system.

25. Type Descriptions

26. How much the client paid.

27. Date the job was promised to be finished.

28. Date and Time when the job was actually started. When finished enter date and time Completed.

29. Click on Save

30. You can open the Service Name to view.

31. Click on the appropriate service link forms if required: https://forms.zoho.com/SRVGroupco/form/TRANSLATIONCERTIFICATE

page.name: Boda-----Wedding-Officiate
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for Marriage Officiation Service

Purpose

To guide representatives through the marriage officiation process, ensuring a clear understanding of each step and the required documentation, particularly for Indiana residents.

Scope

This SOP provides detailed instructions on guiding clients through obtaining a marriage license, preparing for the ceremony, and ensuring all legal and state requirements are met.

Responsibilities

  • Front Desk Staff: Provide detailed information on documentation and scheduling appointments.
  • Officiants: Ensure the ceremony complies with Indiana state laws.
  • Administrative Staff: Assist in application and documentation process, and guide clients through post-ceremony steps.

Procedures

Initial Inquiry and Eligibility Check

1. Direct clients to apply for a marriage license at the Clerk's Office in an Indiana county where at least one party resides or, for out-of-state residents, in the county where the marriage will be solemnized.

2. Verify client's eligibility for marriage in Indiana, including age and identity verification. If applicable, walk-ins are usually welcome; just review the site for changes.

ACCESS SERVICE

3. Schedule an appointment for license application and ceremony, if needed.

Start Your Application »

4. Guide clients to start their marriage license application online to expedite the process.

5. Finalize officiation details, including date, time, and specific requirements.

Documentation and Application for Marriage License

1. Inform clients about the necessary documentation for proving date of birth and identity. It is recommended to take a birth certificate or passport and any valid ID available on the day of the application. Additionally, a confirmation of home address is required, which can be a utility bill or phone service bill. If unavailable, we provide notarized attestation under oath as address confirmation for a separate fee.

Payment for Service

The fee for the service is $200, which includes application assistance, receipt of the certificate, a ceremonial certificate, and up to 5 4x6 photos of the ceremony. The service is currently only available in office.

Marriage License Fee

1. Detail the marriage license fee structure, highlighting the difference in fees for Indiana residents and out-of-state applicants, as well as costs for certified copies of the marriage license.

Pre-Ceremony Preparation

1. Encourage clients to complete the marriage license application online and gather all necessary documentation prior to their appointment.

Day of the Ceremony

1. Confirm that the clients have obtained a valid marriage license and have all necessary documentation ready for the ceremony.

Post-Ceremony Follow-Up

1. Guide couples on using their Certified Marriage License for name changes, updating legal documents, and other necessary post-marriage steps.

Additional Notes

- Highlight the importance of checking with the local clerk's office for any specific county requirements or documentation needed beyond the general state requirements.

Approval

This SOP is approved by [Approval Authority Name], ensuring compliance with Indiana state laws and regulations for marriage officiation.

page.name: Document-Examples
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
Document Examples

Welcome to the Document Examples section of our wiki. This resource is designed to provide you with a comprehensive collection of document samples across a wide range of subjects. These documents serve as valuable reference materials to guide and assist you in various tasks and projects.

In this section, you will find examples of:

  • Business and administrative documents
  • Technical and operational manuals
  • Marketing and sales templates
  • Legal and compliance-related documents
  • Human resources forms and policies

Whether you're drafting a report, creating a presentation, or setting up a new process, these document examples can serve as a starting point, offering insights into best practices, formatting, and content organization. Feel free to explore and utilize these resources to enhance the quality and efficiency of your work.

page.name: Example-----Driving-Policy
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
Driving Policy

THE COMPANY NAME seeks to safeguard its employees and others when driving a motor vehicle is required while conducting company business. As an employee of the Company, you will be required to drive from client location to client location.

Agreement

As an employee for the company, I, (Employee name)

_______________________________________________,

hereby agree to the following:

  • I have read and understand the Driver Policy. I agree to comply with the standards and procedures set forth in the said document and with all other guidelines established from time to time for my position.
  • I will attend all scheduled meetings.
  • I will use due care and caution in the operation of my vehicle and will strictly comply with all safe driving rules of the road, including all speed limits, posted directional signs, and parking regulations. Under no circumstances will I operate my vehicle while under the influence of drugs or alcohol or when my physical or mental condition may be otherwise impaired.
  • The vehicle I use to travel between job sites will be maintained in good condition and repair, comply with all rules and regulations governing safe and lawful operation, and comply with all the guidelines established by my employer.
  • Attached to this Agreement is a true and complete copy of my current DMV report (if available). I agree to immediately notify you if I am involved in any accidents or receive any subsequent citations during my employment.
  • I understand that any violation of this Agreement or of any of the standards, procedures, or guidelines applicable to my position may result in suspension or termination. I acknowledge the need for utmost safety and due care in the operation of my vehicle and in the conduct of providing services.
  • I understand that I am prohibited from carrying any passengers without prior management authorization.
  • I agree to only operate the vehicle identified on the vehicle inspection forms submitted to management and will not drive a substitute vehicle without management prior approval.
  • I agree to maintain my automobile insurance (including coverage for Bodily Injury, Property Damage, and Personal Liability) AT ALL TIMES while using my vehicle and will inform my employer if my insurance coverage is changed, canceled, or not renewed. I have provided proof of insurance currently in force.
  • I understand that MY insurance is responsible if I am involved in an accident that causes injury or damage to another person and/or their property. I am also aware that neither my employer nor his insurance will cover my vehicle for any damage, whatsoever.
  • I will not use a cell phone or any other mobile device, while operating a vehicle. If my employer requests I use one, I agree to only use it when it is safe to do so and when my vehicle is in Park.
  • I agree to provide my Manager a current MVR Report every 12 months.

Motor Vehicle Report Release and Authorization

In connection with my application for employment with you, I understand that a motor vehicle report containing public record information concerning my driving record may be requested. I have the right to request the nature and substance of all information contained in my record including the source of this information and the recipients of my record.

I HEREBY CONSENT TO YOUR OBTAINING MY MOTOR VEHICLE RECORD AND I AUTHORIZE WITHOUT RESERVATION, ANY PARTY OR AGENCY CONTACTED TO FURNISH THE ABOVE INFORMATION.

If hired, this authorization shall remain on file and shall serve as ongoing authorization for you to procure my motor vehicle record at any time during my employment period.

Driver’s Name: ________________________________________

Driver License# _____________________ State: _____ Driver’s DOB: ____________________

Driver’s Signature: _____________________________

Required Documents

  • Signed Driving Policy
  • Valid DMV Motor Vehicle Report (MVR) – Dated within last 30 days.
  • Valid Insurance Identification Card for vehicle(s) used by insured.
  • Vehicle Inspection (below)

Vehicle Inspection Record

(Required for Employee-Owned Vehicles)

Driver’s Name: __________________________________________________

Please fill in the vehicle description & information section then indicate the condition of each item listed. A vehicle inspection should be completed at least once every six months or more frequently. All defects and needs should be explained in the comments section. UNDER NO CIRCUMSTANCES ATTEMPT TO DRIVE A VEHICLE WHICH IS IN ANY WAY DEFECTIVE. See your manager or supervisor at once.

Vehicle Description – Registration & Insurance Information...

Driving Policy - Page 2 of 2

page.name: Carta-de-Viajar-----Permiso-de-Menor
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
Child Travel Authorization Letter SOP

Standard Operating Procedure (SOP): Child Travel Authorization Letter

Purpose:

To provide a clear and efficient process for preparing, completing, and notarizing a Child Travel Authorization Letter.

Scope:

This SOP applies to all cases where a Child Travel Authorization Letter is requested.

Procedure:

  1. Receiving Request:
    • Client requests the preparation of a Child Travel Authorization Letter.
    • Record the client’s contact information and any specific requirements or details they provide.
  2. Information Gathering:
    • Provide the client with a form or template to fill out the necessary details for the letter.
    • Ensure the following details are collected:
      • Parent(s) or Legal Guardian(s) Name(s)
      • Minor's Name
      • Date(s) of Travel
      • Departure Location
      • Destination
      • Flight/Transport Details
      • Accompanying Adult's Name
      • Parent/Guardian Contact Details
  3. Drafting the Letter:
    • Use the provided information to complete the Child Travel Authorization Letter.
    • Ensure all sections are filled accurately.
  4. Review and Approval:
    • Send the draft to the client for review.
    • Make any required revisions based on client feedback.
  5. Finalization:
    • Once approved, prepare the final document for signing.
    • Inform the client that they need to sign the document in the presence of a notary.
  6. Notarization:
    • Schedule an appointment with the client for notarization.
    • During the appointment, verify the identity of the signatory(ies).
    • Witness the signing of the document.
    • Notarize the document by affixing your seal and signature.
  7. Record Keeping:
    • Keep a record of the notarized document as per legal and office requirements.
    • Provide the client with the original notarized letter and a copy for their records.
  8. Client Instructions:
    • Instruct the client to keep a copy of the letter with the child’s belongings and another with the adult who accompanies the child.
    • Advise them on any travel regulations or additional documents that might be needed.
  9. Follow-Up:
    • Offer assistance for any additional queries or services they might need related to the travel.

Documentation:

Maintain records of all communications, drafts, and final documents in the client's file.

Pricing:

Refer to the master price list for charging the client. The service is similar to "Doc Prep. Level 3 - Intermediate" with a fee of $80.00.

Additional Notes:

Ensure all information is up-to-date and complies with current travel and legal requirements. Maintain confidentiality and privacy of client information.

Review:

This SOP should be reviewed annually or as needed to ensure compliance with current laws and travel regulations.

page.name: Translations-Services
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
### **Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for Translations**

**Objective:** To streamline the translation process ensuring accuracy, efficiency, and client satisfaction.

**Scope:** This SOP applies to all translation services provided by SRVGROUP.CO.

**Procedure:**

1. **Appointment Scheduling and Service Addition:**
Service Appointments (zoho.com)
   - Ensure the client has an appointment scheduled in the system.
   - Add the translation service to their appointment details.

2. **Pricing and Job Sheet Creation:**
   - Refer to the price list: Translations start at $60, then $25 per sheet.
   - For bulk translations, consider negotiating a discount.
   - Create a job sheet to track the service and ensure proper billing.

3. **Document Preparation and Upload:**
   - Request the client to provide the documents for translation.
   - Ensure documents are uploaded as high-quality, clear images (not PDFs) to the appointment or job sheet for OCR recognition.
   - Name each document appropriately for easy identification.

4. **Form Submission for Translation Service:**
   - Direct the assigned translator to click on the form link for translation services.
   - Ensure they fill out the necessary information, with most fields pre-filled.
   - Attach the document to the form and submit.

5. **Translation Process:**
   - Once the form is received, start the translation.
   - Utilize OCR technology (like Adobe or Word) for initial translation.
   - Manually verify and update the translation for accuracy.
   - Save the translated document as an image or PDF.

6. **Inclusion of Translation Certificate:**
   - Include a translation certificate with the completed work.
   - Use the provided form link to generate and sign the certificate.
   - Scan and attach the signed certificate to the job sheet.

7. **Finalizing and Notifying the Client:**
   - Update the job sheet with completion time and other necessary fields.
   - Notify the client that the translation is ready for pickup.
   - Place the documents in an envelope and store them in the 'work completed' bin.

8. **Translation Package:**
   - The final package should include:
     - The original document or a copy.
     - The translated document.
     - The translation certificate.

**End of Procedure**

---

For your considerations:
- Ensure all steps are clear and easy to follow for every team member.
- Consider using a checklist format for easier tracking during the translation process.
- Regularly review and update the SOP as needed, especially pricing and technology tools.

page.name: Indiana-BMV-Plates-and-Title-Registration-Detailed-SOP
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
Indiana BMV Plates and Title Registration Detailed SOP

Indiana BMV Plates and Title Registration Detailed SOP

1. Initial Preparation
  • Understand registration timeline: Register new vehicles within 45 days of purchase and within 60 days of becoming a new resident.
    • Ensure the client understands the following: 
      • The state legally has up to 45 business days before they have to provide a temporary tag. If they currently have a temp tag that will expire before the 45 day period but need to be able to use the car they have the following Options.
        • Request form the dealer that they renew their temp tag (they are not required to do so but you can ask and some are willing to help)
        • Park the car and do no operate until your plates are received.
        • Operate the vehicle at your own risk, make sure you have insurance and a copy of the plate application or other documents to prove the car is yours and this can help prevent additional legal issues for operating the vehicle with expired tags. 
  • Prepare for address: Provide two proofs of address during the transaction.
    • The two documents must match the purchaser on the application/title and must be dated recently (30 days), we recommend a utilitiy bill, phone bill or other general service bill. If you have car insurance you can use this as one proof. If you are doing this under a company (EIN 'federal') if your EIN letter does not have the current address you must provide another proof which if updated they can use their certified copies from the Indiana SOS INBIZ Site that are instant, in fact if needed you can update the address from the site first them print the certificate.
  • Ensure vehicle titling: The vehicle must be titled before registration.
    • This takes place at the same time of app, ensure the title is correctly filled out. 
2. Document Collection
  • SRV Cover Page or SRV Checklist or BOTH when Available.
    • This helps keep the process more organized and also presents a professional look to our partners at essence and also with our clietns. 
  • Title or Application for Title: Ensure it has barcodes from the BMV.
    • 'Barcodes' - you will only see these when the car was purchase from a dealer and have already send the title request to the bmv, they will not need to have the physical title. In some rare cases if it IS from a dealer and not code is there, create the online account and see if the title is listed under the person or company. If not you CAN NOT proceed until they client returns to the dealer to correct the issues. 
  • Bill of Sale: Optional, serves as proof of purchase.
  • Affidavit of Sales Tax: Indicates whether sales tax was paid. (If Dealer Bought)
  • Notarized Power of Attorney: If a third party is handling registration.
    • We can notarize the document for the agency, otherwise leave blank but ensure we have client signature for our partners to notarize. 
  • For Business: EIN Letter (Required), Company Registration (Required - DO NOT ACCEPT ADMIN DISSOLVED).
  • For Personal: SS Card, ID, Two Proofs of Residence.
    • ONLY GET COPY OF SS CARD AND ID IF THE PERSON HAS A SSN otherwise if not plating under their SSN but using their EIN, you will not need them. 
    • IF YES, in fact they are plating under their own name we will need a copy of their ssn and valid ID but the process usually take the full 45 days or longer, we dont know why they always take so long with SSNs but without fail there are delays every time we turn one in. 
  • Proof of Insurance: Mandatory for all vehicles.
  • Convenience Fee Form: If any convenience fees are applicable.
3. Registration Process
  • Complete the License Plate Form: Fill in relevant vehicle information.
  • Fill 'SRVPlates' in CRM: For record-keeping, automated notifications, tasks and coordination with Masters Group.
  • Business Client Considerations: Register the business with the secretary of state and obtain a Federal EIN. (If Needed - Separate Service)
  • Enter details in CRM: Document the registration process.
  • Partnered Business Procedure: Submit the completed package to Masters Group.
4. Finalizing the Process
  • Create PO, Bill, and Make Payment.
  • Retrieve Temporary Plates: From Masters Group for the client.
  • Payment and Retrieval of Metal Plates: Pay Masters Group and collect the plates.
  • Provide Plates to Client: Hand over the actual plates.
  • Update Google Sheet and Close Work Item: Maintain accurate records.
5. Post-Completion
  • Client Receives Title: Sent directly to their address on file.
6. Additional Considerations
  • Kiosk Method: Use for faster sticker retrieval, with a charge of $40 + BMV fees.
  • Online Registration: Available at myBMV.com, FSP/PSP locations, BMV branches, and BMV Connect Kiosks.

page.name: Sticker-Renewal
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
Standard Operating Procedure for Sticker or Registration Removal for Vehicles

Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for Sticker or Registration Removal for Vehicles (Updated)

Objective: To establish a clear and compliant process for sticker or registration removal for both individuals and businesses, ensuring all clients are fully informed of the services, charges, and associated risks through a mandatory service agreement.

1. Preliminary Information Collection

For Individuals:

  • Collect the client's Social Security Number (SSN).
  • Obtain a valid, active state ID or driver's license. Note: Services cannot proceed with an expired ID.

For Businesses:

  • Acquire the full Employer Identification Number (EIN).
  • Confirm the ability to access the business's record with the state. If there's a change in address, it's possible to send correspondence to a temporary address, to be added to the account.

2. Verification Process

Navigate to the Bureau of Motor Vehicles (BMV) Website.

Use the Trust ID and the EIN to conduct a search for the client's record.

Create an account for the client with the Trust ID and EIN, enabling them to access and review their records online.

3. Registration Renewal Process

Online Renewal: Typically requires 2-4 weeks for mail receipt. This option represents a simple bill payment service.

In-Person Renewal: For expedited service, in-person BMV visits are necessary, incurring additional charges.

4. Service Agreement Requirement

Before proceeding with any service, it is mandatory to have a service agreement on file. This agreement will:

  • Outline the Included Services: Clearly describe what the sticker or registration removal service entails.
  • Detail the Charges: Provide a comprehensive breakdown of all fees, including the standard service charge and any BMV charges or additional fees for expedited service.
  • Explain the Risks: Inform the client of any risks involved, such as potential delays or complications due to incorrect or outdated information.
  • Ensure Understanding and Agreement: Confirm that the client fully understands the service, charges, and risks before any work begins.

5. Service Charges

A $100 fee is applicable for the BMV registration renewal service, plus BMV charges if successful. If unsuccessful due to client-provided misinformation, a partial refund of $50 is issued, retaining $50 for the service attempt.

6. Refund Policy

The refund policy is detailed in the service agreement, explaining the conditions under which refunds are issued and highlighting the non-refundable portion as compensation for time and effort.

Conclusion

This SOP ensures that all clients receive consistent, compliant service with a full understanding of what they are agreeing to. The mandatory service agreement is a critical component of this process, providing transparency and protecting both the client and the service provider.

Note: Customize this SOP to align with your business's specific procedures, legal requirements, and service offerings. Always consult with a legal professional when drafting service agreements to ensure compliance with applicable laws and regulations.

page.name: Mexican-Consulate-Appointment-Process
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
Mexican Consulate Appointment Process

Introduction

This article provides an in-depth guide to the Mexican Consulate Appointment Process. It covers the stages involved, from client intake to finalizing appointments, and outlines the key steps within each stage.

Objective / Purpose

The objective of this article is to equip readers with a comprehensive understanding of the Mexican Consulate Appointment Process. By the end of this guide, readers should be able to efficiently navigate each stage, ensuring a smooth appointment scheduling process.

Background Information

Before diving into the process, it's essential to have some background knowledge. This process is designed to assist clients in securing appointments with the Mexican Consulate. It involves collecting client information, creating email and consulate accounts, checking for appointments, and finalizing the process.

Methodology / Steps

Stage 1: Intake

  • Identify

    • Check in the client following SOP using the Scheduler or Calendar, ensuring the client has a contact record in CRM and that it is up to date.

  • Qualify

    • Create the service case or new record in the Consulate Appointment Module or select it in the New Case. Review the requirements with the client.

  • Details

    • Collect the following client details:

    • Full name
    • DOB
    • PHONE
    • EMAIL
    • Address
    • Emergency contact name, phone, address (if different)
    • Mother and father's full names
    • Place of birth
    • Person who registered them when they were born
    • Reason for the appointment
    • Current IDs on hand
    • Have they ever received a passport or matricula
    • CURP # & birth certificate - this can be looked up using the above info
  • Agreement

    • Highlight the main points of the agreement for the service:

    • NO REFUNDS
    • THE FEE IS FOR OUR TIME
    • APPOINTMENTS ARE FREE; THEY CAN DO IT THEMSELVES, HENCE WHY OUR FEE IS FOR OUR TIME AND NOT THE APPOINTMENT
    • NO GUARANTEE
    • PLEASE NOTIFY US IF YOU GET AN APPOINTMENT ANOTHER WAY SO WE CAN REMOVE YOU FROM THE LIST.
    • ONE RESCHEDULE INCLUDED; AFTER 2, YOU MUST START OVER AND PAY AGAIN
  • Payments

    • Get payment for services.

Consulta e Impresión de la CURP - Mexican Consulate

Consulta e Impresión de la CURP - Mexican Consulate

Overview

This guide provides a summary of the process for consulting and printing the Clave Única de Registro de Población (CURP) via the Mexican Government's website.

Steps for CURP Consultation

Paso 1: Búsqueda (Search)

  • Method 1: Enter CURP code if known.
  • Method 2: Provide personal name and birth data.

Paso 2: Descargar CURP (Download CURP)

Follow instructions to download the CURP after searching.

Documents and Requirements

  • No Documents Required: However, it's important to have identity proof details handy.

Costs

  • Trámite (Procedure): Free of charge.

Options to Complete the Procedure

  • Online: Procedure can be completed online here.
  • Browser Recommendation: Any browser except Internet Explorer for a better experience.
  • In-person: Check here for the nearest citizen service modules.
  • Telephone Assistance: Call 55 5128 0000 (extensions 15103, 15104, 15690, 15692, and 15693) or 800 911 1111, Monday to Friday, 08:00 to 16:00 hours, for any queries.

Additional Information

Secretaría de Gobernación: This service is offered by the Secretaría de Gobernación.

Purpose: Essential for government procedures, services, or program benefits.

CURP | Trámites | gob.mx (www.gob.mx)

Note: This summary is intended for internal use within SRVGROUP.CO for reference. For further assistance, please

page.name: Mexico-POAs
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
Mexican POA Service Process Map

Mexican POA Service Process Map

1. Information Gathering
  • Use a detailed checklist to gather all necessary information from the client. This may include personal details, specific powers to be granted, and other relevant information.
  • Ensure that the information is complete and accurate to prevent any issues in the POA process.
2. Payment Processing
  • Take payment for the POA service. This can be done through various means such as online payment, bank transfer, or in-person transactions.
  • Provide a receipt or confirmation of payment to the client.
3. Signed Service Agreement
  • Ensure the client has signed the service agreement, this is required for all POAs. This can be done on paper using a printed version or online using zoho sign. Once signed ensure you add the SRVID number then scan and upload to their profile documents or the service agreement section. link: https://workdrive.srvgroup.co/writer/open/jsb1qefc84bf1b1774ebcb5b8f3125f6df065
  • Provide a receipt or confirmation of payment to the client.
4. Constructing the POA Letter
  • Use a standard template for the POA letter where possible. Ensure that the template complies with Mexican legal requirements.
  • Customize the letter based on the client's specific needs and information gathered.
5. Client Approval
  • Send the draft POA letter to the client for review and approval.
  • Make any necessary revisions based on the client's feedback to ensure that the document meets their needs and expectations.
6. Notarization
  • Once approved, get the POA letter notarized. This usually involves signing the document in the presence of a notary public.
  • Ensure that the notarization process is conducted according to Mexican legal standards.
7. Apostille (If Needed)
  • If the POA is to be used internationally or requires further legal backing, obtain an apostille for the document.
  • The apostille process involves getting an official certification that the notarization is valid. It's important for international recognition of the document.

page.name: Mexico-POAs-Divorces
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content:
Mexican POA for Divorce
Click this link for the fillable POA document.

Divorce in Mexico

The law that governs divorce in Mexico is the Federal Civil Code and it is accompanied by the relevant State Codes of Civil Procedures that are largely similar but they can differ from one state to another. In addition to these local laws, other provisions may prove relevant, as the case may be, including but not limited to the Migration Law.

A civil marriage is the only one recognized by law in Mexico, therefore it can be dissolved under certain circumstances and provided that the parties are either Mexican residents or they have registered a marriage certificate issued in another country with the Mexican Civil Registry.

There are certain aspects to consider before commencing the process of divorce in Mexico and these include, among others, the manner in which property will be divided under the common regime as well as the treatment of minor children if there are any.

Specialized legal aid, such as that offered by our Mexican lawyers can be important during all of the divorce phases, starting with the initial stages. Expat couples can benefit further from requesting our services as for the spouse who is not a Mexican resident the process may seem complicated at first and the legal proceedings may even deter him or her from starting the process altogether. Our lawyers also provide legal services to the aspirants who are interested to immigrate to Mexico.

What are the grounds for divorce in Mexico?

  • Irrevocable differences: this can be a common ground for divorce in Mexico and it implies that neither of the parties is expressly at fault for the failure of the marriage;
  • Illegality: when one or both spouses are not yet of the legal marriage age of 16;
  • Family ties: when the spouses are related/they have a blood relation;
  • Violence: when one of the parties was subjected to repeated physical or psychological abuse of various degrees;
  • Adultery: when one of the spouses has had a sexual relationship outside the marriage.

A separate situation is the judicial one in which one of the spouses may ask the court to suspend the obligation to cohabit if he can prove that the spouse has an incurable disease (contagious or hereditary), suffers from a mental disorder or in other cases. Understanding the grounds for this type of separation can be important in some cases and our lawyers in Mexico can help you with more information if the obligation to cohabit is the only one you are interested to interrupt. We remind you that in this case the other obligations such as the financial contributions are not suspended.

What are the types of divorce procedures in Mexico?

  • Mutual consent: this is a common form of marriage dissolution and the former spouses both agree that this is the optimal solution; the subsequent arrangements are negotiated and one of our lawyers in Mexico can assist during this stage;
  • Necessary divorce: when the two spouses are subject to a disagreement that can be a solution for disagreeing couples with no children and it can be filed for unilaterally;
  • Administrative divorce: similar to the mutual agreement one, it is often a solution in case of an absolute or partial separate property regime.

It is important to note that a couple can file for divorce after one year of marriage in most cases.

Child Custody in Mexico after Divorce

After a divorce in Mexico, generally, the child goes under the mother’s custody if he/she is under seven years unless there is a severe risk to the child in this situation. 94% of custody decisions were granted to women as per the 2006 report prepared for the Federal District Human Rights Commission (Comision de Derechos Humanos del Distrito Federal, CDHDF). If the parents cannot take care of the child, custody will be granted to grandparents. In Mexico, federal legislation distinguishes between custody and parental authority. Custody denotes the personal supervision of an incompetent adult or a minor child. In the custody situation, it is understood that the ex-spouse who takes on this obligation is entitled to child support from the other ex-spouse. Whereas parental authority refers to the whole group of powers, which also involve duties conferred upon those who exercise them, such as parents, grandparents, or in exceptional cases, adoptive parents.

If you face any problems regarding child custody after filing for divorce in Mexico, you can get legal help from our law firm. Our lawyers in Mexico will explain to you all you need to know about custody or parental authority.

What is Parental Authority?

After a Mexico divorce, if the children are involved, the parental authority is automatically granted to both parents. But suppose a child’s safety or health is at risk due to the violence. In that case, parents may lose custody rights, and the child’s custody will be granted to only one parent or second-degree blood relatives, including siblings, grandparents, uncles, or aunts. The proceedings for parental authority after filing for divorce in Mexico consist of the following steps:

  • The plaintiff files a lawsuit against the defendant, due to which the court will issue summons;
  • That summons has to be delivered in person and signed by the defendant;
  • In return, the defendant responses to the call and provides evidence;
  • The local Mexican court demands and assesses formal proof from both parties;
  • The plaintiff and the defendant then contest the evidence and argue their case;
  • In the end, the judge considers his verdict and makes a ruling.

The parental authority can be lost by judicial resolution as per Article 444 of the Civil Code of the Federal District due to the following reasons:

  • When an individual exercising parental authority is expressly sentenced to loss of that right;
  • In cases of domestic violence committed against a child;
  • If divorce happens, the provisions of Article 283 of this code will come into action.

A common-law partner or a spouse may recover parental authority by proving fulfillment of imposed obligations on him/her. You can consult with our lawyers in Mexico if you have lost your parental control and want to regain it. They will legally help you in acquiring parental authority in Mexico.

Necessary Documents for Custody Proceedings

The applicant needs to submit the following documents to initiate custody proceedings:

  • Original marriage certificate (if the couple is married);
  • Original birth certificate of the child;
  • Address of the parent requesting custody;
  • Address and names of two witnesses who are about to provide facts about the parent requesting custody of the child.

You can rely on the legal services of our immigration lawyers in Mexico if you want to relocate here. They will help you with the submission of the necessary documentation. In addition, if you are facing problems regarding debt collection in Mexico, our lawyers can also provide you with legal assistance in this matter. Furthermore, if you need legal assistance in other matters, such as moving to Mexico from US, our immigration attorneys are at your disposal.

How to Use a Divorce Decree in Foreign Countries?

After acquiring a divorce in Mexico, the interested person should obtain a certified copy of the decree and authenticate it by the appropriate Mexican government authorities. The lawyers at our law firm in Mexico will assist you in the authentication process of divorce decree.

Suppose an individual uses this document in a foreign country, such as in the United States. In that case, the American consular officer will authenticate the Mexican Government official’s signature appearing on the decree. The decree must also be translated by an official translator, who will certify his translation in front of an American consular office. The consular fees for taking the oath of the translator and authentication are $55.00. If you intend to use your divorce decree in any foreign state, you can get legal help from our attorneys in Mexico.

How Can Our Attorneys in Mexico Help You?

Irrespective of whether or not the spouses mutually agree to the divorce or only one of them is willing to terminate the marriage, the divorce process can be a troubling time and a period during which the need for specialized assistance is strongly felt.

Divorce Statistics in Mexico

  • 123,883 divorces were recorded in Mexico alone in 2016;
  • In 2017, 139,807 divorces were registered in this country;
  • In 2017, about 147,581 divorces took place in Mexico.

¿Como me DIVORCIO desde ESTADOS UNIDOS, si estoy CASADO EN MEXICO?

Si usted se caso en MEXICO, y vive en ESTADOS UNIDOS, y se quiere DIVORCIAR, el tramite de Divorcio se puede llevar a cabo únicamente en MEXICO, DISTRITO FEDERAL, o en la ciudad de la REPUBLICA MEXICANA donde USTE se caso por el CIVIL.

Esto es asi, ya que el ACTA DE MATRIMONIO MEXICANA, esta en el REGISTRO CIVIL de MEXICO.

Sin embargo, si usted no puede salir de ESTADOS UNIDOS, o si no desea viajar a MEXICO para llevar a cabo su DIVORCIO, puede tramitar un PODER NOTARIAL en el CONSULADO, para que un familiar, persona de su confianza o uno de nuestros abogados, lo represente en MEXICO y firme la aceptación del DIVORCIO ante el JUZGADO FAMILIAR del DISTRITO FEDERAL.

Con el PODER NOTARIAL, evitara VIAJES, COSTOS DE HOSPEDAJE, COMIDAS, TAXIS, ETC-

EJEMPLO:

SI usted se caso en el DISTRITO FEDERAL, o en: Aguascalientes, Baja California, Baja California Sur, Campeche, Chiapas, Chihuahua, Coahuila, Colima, Durango, Guanajuato, Guerrero, Hidalgo, Jalisco, Mexico, Michoacan, Morelos, Nayarit, Nuevo Leon, Oaxaca, Puebla, Queretaro, Quintana Roo, San Luis Potosi, Sinaloa, Sonora, Tabasco, Tamaulipas, Tlaxcala, Veracruz, Yucatan, Zacatecas.

Al estar CASADO en Mexico, su DIVORCIO se rige bajo las leyes mexicanas.

Si usted se quiere DIVORCIAR desde ESTADOS UNIDOS DE AMERICA, y su esposa esta CONFORME con el DIVORCIO y puede viajar ella al DISTRITO FEDERAL a firmar el DIVORCIO, podemos llevas a cabo su DIVORCIO, sin que usted salga de ESTADOS UNIDOS.

El JUEZ FAMILIAR le pedirá a usted que FIRME ante el la aceptación del DIVORCIO, para lo cual, lo que se haría es que usted acuda al CONSULADO MEXICANO que le quede mas cercano y pida un PODER NOTARIAL para DIVORCIO.

Este PODER NOTARIAL solo le servirá a su APODERADO para que FIRME EL DIVORCIO Y NADA MAS.

La persona que quede de apoderada debe de ACUDIR EN UNA OCASIÓN AL JUZGADO a firmar por usted la aceptación del DIVORCIO.

Al acudir al CONSULADO, al identificarse como MEXICANO, el CONSULADO MEXICANO no le cuestionara sobre su STATUS MIGRATORIO.

¿Cuanto tarda un tramite de DIVORCIO desde ESTADOS UNIDOS?

Aproximadamente de 3 meses

¿Que DOCUMENTOS obtendría al concluir el tramite de DIVORCIO?

  • Sentencia de Divorcio
  • Acta de Divorcio del Distrito Federal.

¿Que es el PODER NOTARIAL PARA DIVORCIO?

El PODER NOTARIAL PARA DIVORCIO, se tramita en el CONSULADO MEXICANO radicado en ESTADOS UNIDOS DE AMERICA y que este mas cercano a su domicilio.

Es importante saber que antes de acudir al CONSULADO MEXICANO se debe de pedir una cita y los Requisitos que pedirá el CONSULADO MEXICANO, ya que si acude sin cita, no le atenderán.

¿El PODER NOTARIAL para DIVORCIO a quien se le otorga?

El poder notarial para DIVORCIO lo puede usted pedir a favor de un familiar cercano, como pueden ser padres, hermanos, tios o también puede ser a favor de un amigo de confianza.

El apoderado debe de tener su identificación mexicana como credencial para votar o pasaporte mexicano.

El apoderado será llamado al JUZGADO FAMILIAR en el Distrito Federal.

¿El PODER NOTARIAL se puede utilizar para algun otro acto o tramite legal en MEXICO?

NO. El poder notarial para DIVORCIO que le otorga en CONSULADO MEXICANO EN ESTADOS UNIDOS, únicamente sirve para su DIVORCIO, ya que se pone una clausula que indica que solo sirve para el DIVORCIO y nada mas.

¿Puedo tramitar EL PODER PARA DIVORCIO ante un NOTARIO PUBLICO DE ESTADOS UNIDOS?

NO. El poder solo servirá si se pide ante el CONSULADO MEXICANO en los ESTADOS UNIDOS, ya que de lo contrario no le servirá de nada el poder que tramite ante un NOTARIO PUBLICO AMERICANO.

¿Costo del PODER NOTARIAL ante el CONSULADO MEXICANO en los ESTADOS UNIDOS?

115 Dolares Americanos

FUNCION NOTARIAL DE LOS CONSULADOS MEXICANOS EN ESTADOS UNIDOS U.S.A.

La función notarial consular está condicionada a la autorización de actos jurídicos que vayan a ser ejecutados o a tener efectos dentro del territorio de la Republica Mexicana y en la Ciudad de Mexico, Distrito Federal.

Por lo tanto, los documentos notariales expedidos por los Consulados Mexicanos, surten efectos inmediatos, sin necesidad de legalización o protocolización alguna.

PODER NOTARIAL PARA PERSONA FÍSICAS.

El Poder General vale para una serie de actos indefinidos, por lo que el mandatario puede realizar múltiples gestiones en representación del mandante hasta en tanto no sea revocado, o bien, fallezca una de las partes.

Sin embargo, el poder puede ser limitado en su alcance en cuanto a las facultades conferidas, como en el caso de LOS PODERES NOTARIALES PARA DIVORCIO.

Los mandatos o poderes generales pueden ser:

  • Poder General para Pleitos y Cobranzas: Sirve para representar en juicios y efectuar cobros a nombre del mandante.
  • Poder General para Actos de Administración: Sirve para administrar bienes e intereses del mandante.
  • Poder General para Actos de Dominio: Sirve para comprar bienes a nombre del mandante o para venderlos, hipotecarlos o donarlos.

El Poder Especial lo otorga el mandante para ser representado en lo que autoriza de manera expresa en el mandato, pudiendo el mandatario realizar diversas gestiones que estén relacionadas directamente con el objeto del mandato, gestiones que se podrán enunciar en cláusulas.

Este mandato se extingue automáticamente con la conclusión de lo encomendado.

El poder especial, por su mismo carácter, no tiene ningún nombre previo y tiene como ventaja sobre el poder general que proporciona mayor seguridad al mandante, puesto que el mandatario no puede intervenir en otros asuntos que no sean los específicamente determinados.

page.name: Notary-Service
page.author: un75978986562673019r72id
page.content: The email is informing the recipient that their application to become an Indiana Notary Commission has been approved. It emphasizes the importance of being impartial and unbiased in notarizing agreements, and provides a guide on the laws governing notary acts. The recipient is advised to seek legal advice if unsure about any situation. The email also mentions that the recipient may be held liable for negligent or fraudulent acts. The Notary Commission Certificate is attached for the recipient's records, and they are instructed to obtain their official seal from a vendor of their choice. Contact information for further assistance is provided.
